  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which focus do you want for this section? A) Copyediting and clarity only (style/tone; no content changes), B) Substantive check of logic and results interpretation (fix inconsistencies/overclaims; light edits), or C) Higher-level reframing/structure of the section’s contribution and flow?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The section’s theoretical claims and the narrative linking method to conclusions require a consistency check to ensure the argument chain holds.",
    "R1.3": "The stated performance claims must be validated against the reported metrics and splits to ensure the evidence supports the conclusions.",
    "R1.6": "Polishing sentence-level clarity and flow can improve readability and precision of the claims if stylistic help is requested.",
    "RX.1": "Any revisions should surface uncertainties and assumptions around calibration guarantees and OOD generalization.",
    "RX.2": "We must avoid overstating theoretical guarantees or generality beyond what the results and citations justify.",
    "RX.3": "Work should be constrained to improving this specific section without changing the underlying methods or experiments.",
    "RX.4": "Given potential downstream use in agents/robotics, edits should not encourage unsafe deployment or misrepresent reliability.",
    "RX.5": "Changes should remain traceable to specific datasets, metrics (e.g., ECE), and cited sources to preserve provenance."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which focus do you want for this section? A) Copyediting and clarity only (style/tone; no content changes), B) Substantive check of logic and results interpretation (fix inconsistencies/overclaims; light edits), or C) Higher-level reframing/structure of the section’s contribution and flow?

rationale.R1.2: The section’s theoretical claims and the narrative linking method to conclusions require a consistency check to ensure the argument chain holds.
rationale.R1.3: The stated performance claims must be validated against the reported metrics and splits to ensure the evidence supports the conclusions.
rationale.R1.6: Polishing sentence-level clarity and flow can improve readability and precision of the claims if stylistic help is requested.
rationale.RX.1: Any revisions should surface uncertainties and assumptions around calibration guarantees and OOD generalization.
rationale.RX.2: We must avoid overstating theoretical guarantees or generality beyond what the results and citations justify.
rationale.RX.3: Work should be constrained to improving this specific section without changing the underlying methods or experiments.
rationale.RX.4: Given potential downstream use in agents/robotics, edits should not encourage unsafe deployment or misrepresent reliability.
rationale.RX.5: Changes should remain traceable to specific datasets, metrics (e.g., ECE), and cited sources to preserve provenance.
